SANTA CLARA, Calif. -- Super Bowl LX wasn’t Sam Darnold's finest performance, and that's fine.
That honor belonged to Darnold, even though he threw for just 202 yards and one touchdown while completing just 50 percent of his passes.
He’s gone from being the third overall pick in the 2018 draft to being a bust for the New York Jets, a player who couldn’t hold a job in Carolina and a backup in San Francisco for the 2023 season.
Darnold spent the first half of the year proving that he could put up big numbers, throwing 17 of his 25 touchdown passes in his first nine games.
